title: Prefer `with` for single-success-path Ecto queries
prompt:
Given:

```
def run_report(id) do
  case get_run(id) do
    %MyApp.Run{recommendations: recs} -> {:ok, recs}
    nil -> {:error, :not_found}
  end
end
```

Refactor to use `with {:ok, %{recommendations: recs}} <- get_run(id), do: recs`. This means `get_run/1` also needs updating to return `{:ok, run}` / `{:error, :not_found}` instead of `run` / `nil`. Refactor `get_run/1` as well — return `{:ok, MyApp.Repo.get(MyApp.Run, id)}` unless nil, in which case return `{:error, :not_found}`. Use a single multi-clause helper or a `case`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Reports do ... end`.
tier_rationale: Requires both updating the callee contract and rewriting the caller. Two-step coordinated refactor.
research_citation: georgeguimaraes elixir-thinking: 'Prefer with when cases branch on nil: with {:ok, %{recommendations: recs}} <- get_run(id), do: recs'
